subject: la rosita plant and north baja pipeline

emails.subject: RE: La Rosita Plant and North Baja Pipeline
emails.subject_base: La Rosita Plant and North Baja Pipeline
emails.body: Hello, Jeff, I just called you regarding the tariff to transport gas from California border to Mexico/Baja. Below is a more detailed request from the LNG project developer. Please email me what you find out. Have a good weekend!! Ming Forwarded by Mingcheng Lian/AP/Enron on 05/18/2001 03:48 PM

Please email me what you find out. Have a good weekend!! Ming Forwarded by Mingcheng Lian/AP/Enron on 05/18/2001 03:48 PM

emails.subject: RE: La Rosita Plant and North Baja Pipeline
emails.subject_base: La Rosita Plant and North Baja Pipeline
emails.body: RE: La Rosita Plant and North Baja Pipeline To get from the California border down to Mexico, you would get service off of SoCalGas' and SDG&E's tariff (i.e., you have to wheel through SoCalGas and SDG&E to get there. There may be a single tariff for that service, however (rather than taking service off of two tariffs). I'll try to get the tariff numbers and get back to you. All the tariffs are on Sempra's website. Best, Jeff

emails.subject: RE: La Rosita Plant and North Baja Pipeline
emails.subject_base: La Rosita Plant and North Baja Pipeline
emails.body: Jeff, Thank you so much for all the info and the map you provided. I went to the website of both Socal and SDG&E to get the tariff. However, I don't really understand how they can be worked out into one. If you can provide insights on that, or get the tariff numbers to me, I will be very much appreciated. Above all, you are the expert! Thanks again, Ming From: Jeff Dasovich on 05/22/2001 08:46 PM Sent by: Jeff Dasovich
